mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series 420i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 420i engine typically costs £3,200–£7,500 supply only depending on generation, with the current B48B20 sitting at £3,500–£7,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 4 Series 420i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which generation you're asking about. The N20 (F32, pre-2020) has a well-documented timing chain tensioner issue — pre-2016 cars are the highest risk. The B48 (G22, 2020+) is significantly more reliable, with the timing chain moved to the front of the engine. Across both, oil-change discipline is everything.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 420i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20 timing chain wear (the defining weak point of the F32 generation), N20 oil filter housing gasket failure, B48 coolant leaks and PCV system issues, and valve cover gasket failure (both engines). See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 420i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £8,000–£12,000 F32 420i, a £4,000–£4,500 recon is 35–50% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a £20,000+ G22, a £5,000 recon is 25% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 420i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: F32/F33/F36 (2013–2020) = N20B20; G22/G23/G26 (2020–present) = B48B20. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £800–£1,800. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£3,200–£6,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Pre-2016 cars are the highest risk.

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain Tensioner & Guide Failure (F32, pre-2016)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 1–3 seconds, engine warning light, loss of power, metallic noises from the front of the engine, "Drivetrain Malfunction" warning.
problems.replacementCost: £3,200–£6,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N20's timing chain tensioner is the defining weak point of this engine generation. Pre-2016 cars are the highest risk, with the chain guides being prone to failure. BMW revised the tensioner and guides in 2016, but the risk remains on all N20 engines to some degree. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston-valve contact and a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-approved oil are the best prevention. At the first sign of rattle, get it inspected immediately.

problems.title: N20 Oil Filter Housing Gasket Failure
problems.symptoms: Oil leak from the oil filter housing area, oil warning light, low oil pressure, oil on the front of the engine.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can lead to oil starvation

problems.recommendation: The N20's oil filter housing gasket is prone to failure, causing oil leaks that can damage other components if left unaddressed. The gasket becomes brittle and cracks over time. A leaking oil filter housing can also lead to oil starvation, which is catastrophic for the engine. Regular inspections can catch this before it becomes a major issue.

problems.title: B48 Coolant Leaks & PCV System Issues (G22)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning, coolant loss with no visible external leak, sweet smell from engine bay, rough idle, white smoke from exhaust (PCV).
problems.repairCost: £300–£1,200 (coolant system diagnosis and repair, PCV diaphragm repair)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss is detected

problems.recommendation: The B48 is a significant improvement over the N20, but it has its own quirks. Coolant leaks (often from the coolant reservoir, water pump, or charge cooler) are more common than in the N20. The PCV system is also a known weak point, with the valve and diaphragm being prone to failure and causing drivability issues. BMW has released several revised parts for these issues. If you notice coolant loss or rough idle, have the system inspected immediately.

problems.title: Valve Cover Gasket Failure (Both N20 and B48)
problems.symptoms: Oil leak from the top of the engine, burning oil smell, visible oil seepage around the valve cover.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; oil leaks can damage other components

problems.recommendation: Both the N20 and B48 can suffer from valve cover gasket failure. The gasket is made of rubber and has a tendency to become brittle and crack over time due to heat exposure. Obvious oil leaks are the most common symptom. Leaking oil can saturate and degrade vacuum, coolant, and air intake hoses. Regular inspections can catch this before it becomes a major issue.

problems.title: VANOS Solenoid Wear (N20)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, reduced power, check engine light.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect variable valve timing

problems.recommendation: VANOS solenoid failure due to O-ring wear can affect variable valve timing on the N20. If you experience rough idle or hesitation, have the VANOS system inspected promptly.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 420i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals
* N20 cars (pre-2020): timing chain tensioner inspected or replaced (2016+ cars are lower risk)
* B48 cars (2020+): no coolant loss and PCV system functioning correctly
* No cold-start rattle on N20 cars
* B48 cars: no coolant loss or sweet smell from engine bay
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* N20 with cold-start rattle — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* Any coolant loss with no diagnosis — B48 cooling system repairs can be complex
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — the N20 and B48 can hide expensive faults
* Excessive oil consumption — indicates potential piston ring or valve seal issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 420i offers a compelling entry into the 4 Series range — sleek styling, strong performance from the 2.0-litre turbo, and excellent fuel economy. The N20-powered F32 generation is a capable car but demands timing chain vigilance, particularly on pre-2016 examples. The B48-powered G22 generation is a significant step forward in reliability and refinement, with the timing chain moved to the front of the engine. Buy on service history, maintain on a shortened schedule, and this is an excellent premium coupé.

cta.label: → Compare 420i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 420i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle lasting more than 1–2 seconds (N20 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N20/B48) and confirmed oil-change discipline (8k–10k miles max)
* Oil Level — Check dipstick; excessive consumption indicates potential issues
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil around the oil filter housing (N20) or valve cover (both)
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates B48 cooling system issues
* Idle Quality — Any roughness? PCV or VANOS issues may be present
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, cooling, or VANOS codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to fuel system or PCV issues)
